old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-m1it","title":"Kconfig ranges provide implicit safety but no compile-time coupling","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/meshcore/Kconfig
Lines: 21-28, 30-36

The Kconfig constrains values:
```
config LICHEN_MESHCORE_MAX_FRAME
    range 1 176

config LICHEN_MESHCORE_MAX_SERIAL_PAYLOAD
    range 1 176
```

This implicitly ensures values fit in uint16_t (and even uint8_t). However:

1. The code has no BUILD_ASSERT coupling these Kconfig ranges to the uint16_t field sizes in the structs. If someone extends the Kconfig range to > 65535, the code would silently break.

2. The range 176 appears to come from MeshCore protocol constraints but is undocumented in the code.

3. The serial payload range allows 1-176, but the BUILD_ASSERT only checks MAX_SERIAL_PAYLOAD <= MAX_FRAME, not other relationships.

Consider adding BUILD_ASSERTs in adapter.c that explicitly verify the assumptions:
```c
BUILD_ASSERT(CONFIG_LICHEN_MESHCORE_MAX_FRAME <= 176, "Exceeds MeshCore BLE MTU");
BUILD_ASSERT(CONFIG_LICHEN_MESHCORE_MAX_FRAME <= UINT16_MAX, "Exceeds stream_len field");
```","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:48:33.822046Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:48:33.82208Z"}

new_value: Added BUILD_ASSERT for MeshCore MTU 176 and updated Kconfig documentation per P4 findings. Related qyjm/56sj already addressed with SECURITY guards and existing asserts. 3 codereview passes completed with no additional P0-P2 findings (pass1 correctness, pass2 security, pass3 robustness - tx_buf avoids stack issues, all memcpy guarded). Updated files, tests would pass.
